# Debugging Log — Woof-CE Custom Puppy Build

*autogenerated via Claude*

This document records the issues encountered while building a custom Puppy Linux
distribution with Woof-CE inside a Multipass Ubuntu VM (Windows 11 host), targeting
Ubuntu Jammy (22.04) as the package source. It's intended as a reference for
future builds and for anyone else hitting the same chain of failures.

## Environment

- **Host:** Windows 11, 64-bit, Multipass (Ubuntu 26.04 VM)
- **Build target:** `woof-out_x86_64_x86_64_ubuntu_jammy64` (Jammy packages, x86_64)
- **Base Puppy (final, working):** FossaPup64 9.5 (`fossapup64-9.5.iso` +
  `devx_fossapup64_9.5.sfs`)
- **Base Puppy (initial, broken):** Tahr64 6.0.6 — **do not use this as a base
  for a Jammy build, see Issue 1**

---

## Issue 1: Wrong base Puppy (Tahr64) — root cause of most early failures

**Symptom:** `1download` reported `failed (file not found or corrupted file)`
for nearly every package, even though each `.deb` downloaded at 100% with the
correct file size.

**Root cause:** The build was run via `run_woof` using **Tahr64**, a Puppy
release built from Ubuntu Trusty (14.04). Tahr64 ships a 32-bit userland, and
`run_woof` silently wraps the session in `linux32` when it detects this. The
bundled `dpkg-deb` tool inside this old environment predates the package
compression format (`.tar.xz` / zstd-based control archives) that modern
Ubuntu mirrors now serve, even for older suites. `dpkg-deb -c` — used by
`verifypkg.sh` to validate downloads — failed with errors like:

```
dpkg-deb: error: archive 'acl_2.2.53-6_amd64.deb' has premature member
'control.tar.xz' before 'control.tar.gz', giving up
```

**What did *not* work:**
- Swapping in the host VM's modern `dpkg-deb` binary — it required a newer
  glibc/liblzma than the Tahr64-based chroot provided (`GLIBC_2.33` /
  `GLIBC_2.34` not found).
- Installing 32-bit compatibility libraries (`dpkg --add-architecture i386`,
  etc.) — fixed `ldd` resolution outside the chroot, but the actual build
  process runs in a separate, layered chroot that doesn't see host-level
  package installs.

**Fix:** Rebuild from scratch using **FossaPup64 9.5** (Ubuntu Focal-based,
genuinely 64-bit) instead of Tahr64:

```bash
sudo ./run_woof ./fossapup64-9.5.iso ./devx_fossapup64_9.5.sfs .
```

FossaPup64's bundled tooling is recent enough to correctly parse current
Ubuntu package formats, and `0setup` then correctly pulls the 64-bit variant
of Woof-CE's bundled `dpkg-deb` from `woof-arch/x86_64/...` instead of the
32-bit `woof-arch/x86/...` copy.

**Source for FossaPup64:**
- ISO: `http://distro.ibiblio.org/puppylinux/puppy-fossa/fossapup64-9.5.iso`
- DevX SFS: `https://archive.org/download/puppy_linux_-fossapup64/devx_fossapup64_9.5_cmake_included.sfs`

---

## Issue 2: Missing `/tmp/petget_proc` directory

**Symptom:**

```
verifypkg.sh: line 28: /tmp/petget_proc/pgks_failed_to_install_forced: No such file or directory
```

**Root cause:** `verifypkg.sh` writes a failure log to `/tmp/petget_proc/`,
which isn't created automatically in a fresh chroot session.

**Fix:**

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/petget_proc
```

Re-run as needed if this directory doesn't persist across sessions.

---

## Issue 3: busybox source tarball not fetched during `3builddistro-Z`

**Symptom:**

```
sha256sum: can't open 'busybox-1.37.0.tar.bz2': No such file or directory
busybox-1.37.0.tar.bz2: FAILED
```

**Root cause:** Transient — the automated `wget` inside the busybox petbuild
script (`rootfs-petbuilds/busybox/petbuild`) didn't complete during the
scripted run. The URL itself was valid (confirmed by fetching manually).

**Fix:** Manually download the tarball into the build root directory before
re-running the build:

```bash
wget -t 3 -T 60 https://busybox.net/downloads/busybox-1.37.0.tar.bz2
./3builddistro-Z
```

The petbuild script checks `[ -f busybox-1.37.0.tar.bz2 ] || wget ...`, so an
already-present file is used as-is and the build proceeds.

---

## Issue 4: `ccache` broken inside petbuild sandbox (`libhiredis.so.0.14` missing)

**Symptom:**

```
/usr/bin/ccache: error while loading shared libraries: libhiredis.so.0.14: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
ERROR: failed to build busybox
```

This blocked **every** petbuild that compiles from source (busybox, jwm,
rox-filer, etc.), since all of them route through the same compiler wrapper.

**What did *not* work (and why):**
- Renaming/disabling `ccache` inside `sandbox3/devx/usr/bin/` — this directory
  is just one *layer*; the actual compilation happens inside a freshly
  assembled, separate chroot (`petbuild-rootfs-complete-${NAME}`) that gets
  rebuilt per petbuild, silently discarding any manual changes made to
  `sandbox3/devx`.
- Setting `export CCACHE_DISABLE=1` in the shell — the petbuild process runs
  in a chroot invocation that doesn't inherit the host shell's exported
  environment variables.
- Copying a correct `libhiredis.so.0.14` into `sandbox3/devx/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/`
  — same root cause as above: wrong layer, gets bypassed by the real build
  chroot.

**Root cause:** The compiler variables are hardcoded in `support/petbuilds.sh`:

```sh
WOOF_CC="/usr/bin/ccache gcc"
WOOF_CXX="/usr/bin/ccache g++"
```

**Fix:** Edit `support/petbuilds.sh` to drop the `ccache` wrapper:

```bash
cp support/petbuilds.sh support/petbuilds.sh.bak
sed -i 's|WOOF_CC="/usr/bin/ccache gcc"|WOOF_CC="gcc"|' support/petbuilds.sh
sed -i 's|WOOF_CXX="/usr/bin/ccache g++"|WOOF_CXX="g++"|' support/petbuilds.sh
```

Verify:

```bash
grep -n "WOOF_CC\|WOOF_CXX" support/petbuilds.sh
# Expect: WOOF_CC="gcc"  /  WOOF_CXX="g++"
```

---

## Issue 5: False checksum mismatch on petbuild downloads (BusyBox `sha256sum` incompatibility)

**Symptom:**

```
sha256sum: WARNING: 1 of 1 computed checksums did NOT match
```

**Root cause:** Inside the chroot, `/bin/sha256sum` is a BusyBox applet, not
GNU coreutils. BusyBox's `sha256sum -c` is stricter about line format than
GNU's implementation. Even a byte-correct checksum file can fail BusyBox's
parser. This affects any petbuild download — first seen with `gtkdialog`,
later with `transmission`.

**Fix:** Regenerate the checksum file using the same BusyBox `sha256sum` that
will verify it:

```bash
cd petbuild-sources/<pkgname>
sha256sum <tarball> > ../../rootfs-petbuilds/<pkgname>/sha256.sum
cd ../..
./3builddistro
```

Example for gtkdialog:

```bash
cd petbuild-sources/gtkdialog
sha256sum gtkdialog-0.8.5e.tar.gz > ../../rootfs-petbuilds/gtkdialog/sha256.sum
cd ../..
```

> **Note:** Always manually verify the hash actually matches before
> regenerating. If hashes don't match, the download is genuinely corrupted —
> delete and re-download. If they do match, it's a BusyBox format issue and
> regenerating the `.sum` file fixes it.

---

## Issue 6: Petbuild cascade failure due to incorrect `exit 1` patch in `petbuilds.sh`

**Symptom:** After abiword failed to build, every subsequent petbuild also
failed silently — gnumeric, xournalpp, mpv, gparted, deadbeef, and ~15 others.
The final ISO was missing the main `.sfs` file entirely.

**Root cause:** A previous Claude instance patched `support/petbuilds.sh` line
231 to skip abiword's failure:

```bash
sed -i '231s/exit 1/: # skipping failed petbuild, continuing.../' support/petbuilds.sh
```

This replaced `exit 1` with a bash no-op, causing all petbuild failures to be
silently swallowed. The chroot environment was left in a corrupted state after
abiword's failed compile, poisoning every subsequent petbuild.

**Fix:** Revert the patch:

```bash
sed -i '231s/: # skipping failed petbuild, continuing.../exit 1/' support/petbuilds.sh
```

Verify:

```bash
sed -n '229,233p' support/petbuilds.sh
```

`exit 1` should be on line 231. Then address the root cause of whichever
petbuild is actually failing rather than masking errors.

---

## Issue 7: Petbuilds failing due to missing dependencies in chroot (abiword, gnumeric, xournalpp, mpv, gparted, deadbeef)

**Symptom:** Multiple petbuilds fail with missing library errors:

- `abiword` — `boost not found`, `cannot find -lwv`
- `gnumeric` — `Package 'libgsf-1' not found`
- `xournalpp` — `cannot find -lzip`
- `mpv` — `You manually enabled the feature 'libbluray'`
- `gparted` — `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'libxml2'` (in help install)
- `deadbeef` — `relocation R_X86_64_PC32 against symbol stderr can not be used when making a shared object`

**Root cause:** These packages require libraries that are not present inside
the petbuild chroot. Each would require enabling additional entries in
`DISTRO_PKGS_SPECS-ubuntu-jammy` and rerunning `./2createpackages`.

**Fix (recommended):** Remove unwanted apps from `PETBUILDS` in `_00build.conf`
rather than chasing each missing dependency. These are all optional apps that
can be added later as SFS packages:

```bash
sed -i 's/ abiword//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ gnumeric//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ xournalpp//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ mpv//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ gparted//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ deadbeef//' _00build.conf
```

Verify:

```bash
grep 'PETBUILDS' _00build.conf
```

None of the above should appear. Then clean up remnants and rebuild:

```bash
sudo rm -rf sandbox3/petbuild-rootfs-complete-*
sudo rm -rf /tmp/abiword* /tmp/gnumeric* /tmp/xournalpp* /tmp/mpv* /tmp/gparted* /tmp/deadbeef*
./3builddistro
```

---

## Issue 8: `/tmp` partition full, causing partial petbuild downloads

**Symptom:** A petbuild download fails mid-way with:

```
Cannot write to 'transmission-4.0.6.tar.xz' (Success).
transmission-4.0.6.tar.xz: FAILED
```

Followed by a checksum mismatch on the truncated file.

**Root cause:** The Multipass VM was created with only 20G disk, almost all
allocated to `/tmp`. Petbuilds extract large source tarballs into `/tmp`,
filling it completely.

**Fix (disk resize):** Stop the VM and resize from the Windows host:

```bash
multipass stop <vmname>
multipass set local.<vmname>.disk=50G
multipass start <vmname>
```

**Fix (clean up /tmp):** Remove leftover build artifacts:

```bash
sudo rm -rf /tmp/Packages-ubuntu-jammy* /tmp/0setup_xxx* /tmp/ucode.cpio /tmp/microcode /tmp/woof-debdb.in
sudo rm -rf /tmp/abiword* /tmp/gnumeric* /tmp/xournalpp* /tmp/mpv* /tmp/gparted* /tmp/deadbeef* /tmp/transmission* /tmp/gtkdialog*
```

**Fix (corrupted partial download):** If a tarball was partially written before
disk ran out, delete and re-download it:

```bash
rm petbuild-sources/transmission/transmission-4.0.6.tar.xz
cd petbuild-sources/transmission
wget -t 5 -T 120 https://github.com/transmission/transmission/releases/download/4.0.6/transmission-4.0.6.tar.xz
sha256sum transmission-4.0.6.tar.xz > ../../rootfs-petbuilds/transmission/sha256.sum
cd ../..
./3builddistro
```

---

## Issue 9: Output ISO missing `.sfs` — `mksquashfs` zstd compression not supported

**Symptom:** The build completes but the output ISO has no `.sfs` file
(e.g. `puppy_upup64j_22.04.sfs` is missing). The build log shows:

```
mksquashfs: Compressor "zstd" is not supported!
```

The build continues after this failure instead of stopping, so the ISO gets
assembled without the main filesystem SFS.

**Root cause:** `woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf` has the SFS
compression set to zstd:

```bash
SFSCOMP='-comp zstd -Xcompression-level 19 -b 256K -no-exports -no-xattrs'
```

The version of `mksquashfs` available in the build environment does not have
zstd support compiled in. This is a mismatch between the config and the
available tooling.

**Fix:** Switch compression to xz which is universally supported:

```bash
sed -i "s/-comp zstd/-comp xz/" woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
```

Verify:

```bash
grep SFSCOMP woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
# Should show: SFSCOMP='-comp xz -b 256K -no-exports -no-xattrs'
```

Clean up any incomplete output artifacts before rebuilding:

```bash
rm -f woof-output-upup64j-22.04/*.iso woof-output-upup64j-22.04/*.sfs 2>/dev/null
./3builddistro
```

> **Note:** xz produces slightly larger SFS files than zstd but is slower to
> compress and faster to decompress — acceptable tradeoff for a working build.
> If you want zstd back later, install a newer `squashfs-tools` that includes
> zstd support: `sudo apt install squashfs-tools` on the host VM and verify
> with `mksquashfs -version`.

---

## Issue 10: ccache `libhiredis` error in cmake-based petbuilds (transmission, weechat)  

**Symptom:**

```
ccache: error while loading shared libraries: libhiredis.so.0.14: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
gmake[2]: *** [...] Error 127
ERROR: failed to build transmission
ERROR: failed to build weechat
```

**Root cause:** Some petbuilds (transmission, weechat) use cmake internally,
which invokes ccache directly regardless of the `WOOF_CC`/`WOOF_CXX` fix in
`petbuilds.sh`. The cmake build system bypasses the shell-level compiler
variable override and calls `/usr/bin/ccache` directly, hitting the same
missing `libhiredis.so.0.14` error.

**What transmission and weechat are:**
- `transmission` — BitTorrent client
- `weechat` — IRC/chat client

**Fix (recommended):** Remove them from `PETBUILDS` if you don't need them:

```bash
sed -i 's/ transmission//' _00build.conf
sed -i 's/ weechat//' _00build.conf
sudo rm -rf sandbox3/petbuild-rootfs-complete-transmission
sudo rm -rf sandbox3/petbuild-rootfs-complete-weechat
sudo rm -rf /tmp/transmission* /tmp/weechat*
./3builddistro
```

Both can be added later as SFS packages if needed.

---

## Issue 11: `_00build.conf` changes not persisting across `merge2out` regenerations

**Symptom:** Packages removed from `PETBUILDS` in `_00build.conf` reappear
after running `merge2out` to regenerate woof-out.

**Root cause:** There are multiple `_00build.conf` files in woof-CE. The one
that actually governs your build is **not** `woof-code/_00build.conf` — it is
the distro-specific one at:

```
woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
```

When `merge2out` runs, the distro-specific file takes precedence and overwrites
the `woof-code` version in woof-out. Any edits made only to `woof-code/_00build.conf`
are silently ignored for jammy64 builds.

**Fix:** Always edit the correct file:

```bash
# Find all _00build.conf files to confirm which one applies
find woof-CE/ -name "_00build.conf"

# Edit the jammy64-specific one
nano woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
```

Or use sed directly:

```bash
sed -i 's/ abiword//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ gnumeric//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ xournalpp//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ mpv//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ gparted//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ deadbeef//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ transmission//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
sed -i 's/ weechat//' woof-CE/woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf
```

> **Rule of thumb:** For any jammy64 build config change, always edit
> `woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf`, not `woof-code/_00build.conf`.

---

## Issue 12: Kernel download fails — `wget: missing URL` in `huge_kernel.sh`

**Symptom:**

```
Installing HUGE kernel to build/
wget: missing URL
Usage: wget [OPTION]... [URL]...
Try `wget --help' for more options.
Could not get kernel list
If you have connectivity issues (or the site is unreachable)
 place a huge kernel in the 'huge_kernel' directory
Type A in hit enter to retry, any other key to exit
```

Retrying with `A` produces the same error indefinitely.

**Root cause:** A variable name mismatch bug in `support/huge_kernel.sh`. The
script uses `$KERNEL_REPO_URL` on line 117:

```bash
wget ${KERNEL_REPO_URL} -O $TMP
```

But `_00build.conf` only defines `KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL` (with the `KIT_` prefix):

```bash
KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL=http://distro.ibiblio.org/puppylinux/huge_kernels
```

`KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL` is used correctly by the separate `support/kit_kernel.sh`
script, but `huge_kernel.sh` uses a different variable name that never gets set.
So `wget` receives an empty string instead of a URL — hence "missing URL".

This is a bug in woof-CE itself, not a network or configuration issue. Retrying
will never work.

**Fix:** Add an alias line to `huge_kernel.sh` right after it sources the
config files (after line 8), so `KERNEL_REPO_URL` inherits its value from
`KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL`:

```bash
sed -i '8a KERNEL_REPO_URL="${KERNEL_REPO_URL:-$KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL}"' support/huge_kernel.sh
```

Verify it landed correctly:

```bash
sed -n '1,12p' support/huge_kernel.sh
```

You should see the new line after the last `.conf` sourcing line. Then rerun:

```bash
./3builddistro
```

> **Important:** Apply this fix to `woof-CE/woof-code/support/huge_kernel.sh`
> as well so it persists across future `merge2out` regenerations:
>
> ```bash
> sed -i '8a KERNEL_REPO_URL="${KERNEL_REPO_URL:-$KIT_KERNEL_REPO_URL}"' \
>     woof-CE/woof-code/support/huge_kernel.sh
> ```

---

## Quick-start checklist for a fresh Multipass instance

1. **Allocate enough disk** — minimum 50G for the Multipass VM (`multipass launch --disk 50G`).
2. Install build dependencies (`git`, `genisoimage`, `squashfs-tools`, `wget`,
   `xorriso`, `gettext`, `python3`, `cpio`, `bc`).
3. Clone your woof-CE fork (with `petbuilds.sh` ccache fix already committed).
4. Download **FossaPup64 9.5** ISO + matching DevX SFS — do **not** use
   Tahr64 or other pre-Focal-era Puppy bases on a modern host.
5. `sudo ./run_woof ./fossapup64-9.5.iso ./devx_fossapup64_9.5.sfs .`
6. `mkdir -p /tmp/petget_proc` (inside the chroot, each session).
7. Run `./merge2out`, select **jammy64**.
8. `cd` into the generated `woof-out_*` directory and run `./0setup`,
   `./1download`, `./2createpackages`, `./3builddistro` in order.
9. If `petbuilds.sh` doesn't already have the ccache fix, apply it before
   running `./3builddistro` (see Issue 4).
10. If any petbuild halts with `WARNING: 1 of 1 computed checksums did NOT match`,
    manually verify the hash before assuming the download is bad. If hashes
    match, regenerate the `.sum` file in-chroot (see Issue 5).
11. If `/tmp` fills up mid-build, clean it and resize the VM disk (see Issue 8).
12. Do not use the `exit 1` bypass patch in `petbuilds.sh` — it causes silent
    cascade failures across all petbuilds (see Issue 6).
13. Change `SFSCOMP` from zstd to xz in `woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf`
    before building — otherwise the ISO will be missing its `.sfs` (see Issue 9).
14. Always edit `woof-distro/x86_64/ubuntu/jammy64/_00build.conf` for PETBUILDS
    changes — not `woof-code/_00build.conf` (see Issue 11).
15. Apply the `huge_kernel.sh` KERNEL_REPO_URL fix before running `./3builddistro`
    (see Issue 12).
